rulings.Case Title: MINGFEI LI ET AL VS. JOHN L. YI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 14, 2023 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to November 27, 2023 to be hearing with other pending motions =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ET AL VS. 320 ALEMANY LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of A Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 14, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO,, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, NOTICE OF A MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES is GRANTED as follows: (1) Defendant to provide further responses, subject to protective order; (2) parties to meet and confer on the form of protective order and present the order for execution at the hearing; (3) if parties cannot agree on the form of order, parties to prepare an order including the language suggested by the Plaintiff in bold; the language suggested by Defendant in italics, and the agreed-upon language in standard font. This "hybrid" order to be presented to the Court by 9:00 a.m. on November 14, 2023; (4) Defendant to provide responses within 20 days of notice of entry of order granting this motion, subject to protective order =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
